nodes.name: CA
nodes.description: This cryptographic store contains intermediary certification authorities.

children.description: The SHA1 hash for the certificate. The 20-byte SHA1 hash of the certificate is specified in hexadecimal. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: EncodedCertificate
children.description: The base64 Encoded X.509 certificate

children.name: IssuedBy
children.description: The name of the certificate issuer. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: IssuedTo
children.description: The name of the certificate subject. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: TemplateName
children.description: Returns the certificate template name.

children.name: ValidFrom
children.description: The starting date of the certificate's validity. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: ValidTo
children.description: The expiration date of the certificate. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: System
children.description: This store holds the System portion of the CA store.
The SHA1 hash for the certificate. The 20-byte SHA1 hash of the certificate is specified in hexadecimal. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: EncodedCertificate
children.description: The base64 Encoded X.509 certificate.

children.name: IssuedBy
children.description: The name of the certificate issuer. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: IssuedTo
children.description: The name of the certificate subject. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: TemplateName
children.description: Returns the certificate template name.

children.name: ValidFrom
children.description: The starting date of the certificate's validity. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: ValidTo
children.description: The expiration date of the certificate. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

nodes.name: MY
nodes.description: This store keeps all end-user personal certificates.

children.name: SCEP
children.description: This store holds the SCEP portion of the MY store and handle operations related to SCEP certificate enrollment.
The UniqueID for the SCEP enrollment request. Each client certificate should have different unique ID.

children.name: CertThumbPrint
children.description: Specify the current cert’s thumbprint.

children.name: ErrorCode
children.description: Specify the last hresult in case enroll action failed.

children.name: Install
children.description: The group to represent the install request

children.name: CAThumbPrint
children.description: Specify root CA thumbprint.

children.name: Challenge
children.description: Enroll requester authentication shared secret.

children.name: EKUMapping
children.description: Specify extended key usages. The list of OIDs are separated by plus “+”.

children.name: Enroll
children.description: Start the cert enrollment.

children.name: HashAlgrithm
children.description: Client create Cert enroll request, get supported hash OIalgorithm from SCEP server  and  match it with one specified in this parameter.

children.name: KeyLength
children.description: Specify private key length (RSA).

children.name: KeyProtection
children.description: Specify where to keep the private key.

children.name: KeyUsage
children.description: Specify the key usage bits (0x80, 0x20, 0xA0) for the cert.

children.name: RetryCount
children.description: When the SCEP sends pending status, specify device retry times.

children.name: RetryDelay
children.description: When the SCEP server sends pending status, specify device retry waiting time in minutes.

children.name: ServerURL
children.description: Specify the cert enrollment server.

children.name: SubjectAlternativeNames
children.description: Specify subject alternative name. Multiple alternative names could be specified by this node. Each name is the combination of name format+actual name. Each pair is separated by semi-comma.

children.name: SubjectName
children.description: Specify the subject name.

children.name: TemplateName
children.description: Certificate Template Name OID (As in AD used by PKI infrastructure.

children.name: ValidPeriod
children.description: Specify the period of time that cert is valid. The valid period specified by MDM will overwrite the valid period specified in cert template.

children.name: ValidPeriodUnit
children.description: Specify valid period unit type.

children.name: Status
children.description: Specify the latest status for the certificate due to enroll request.

children.name: User
children.description: This store holds the User portion of the MY store.
The SHA1 hash for the certificate. The 20-byte SHA1 hash of the certificate is specified in hexadecimal. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: EncodedCertificate
children.description: The base64 Encoded X.509 certificate. Note that though during MDM enrollment, enrollment server could use WAP XML format to add public part of MDM client cert via EncodedCertificate node, properly enroll a client certificate including private needs a cert enroll protocol handle it  or user installs it manually. In WP, the server cannot purely rely on CertificateStore CSP to install a client certificate including private key.

children.name: IssuedBy
children.description: The name of the certificate issuer. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: IssuedTo
children.description: The name of the certificate subject. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: TemplateName
children.description: Returns the certificate template name.

children.name: ValidFrom
children.description: The starting date of the certificate's validity. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: ValidTo
children.description: The expiration date of the certificate. This node is implicitly created only when the EncodedCertificate node is added.

children.name: WSTEP
children.description: The parent node that hosts  client certificate that is enrolled via WSTEP, e.g. the certificate that is enrolled during MDM enrollment.

children.name: CertThumprint
children.description: The thumb print of enrolled MDM client certificate.

children.name: Renew
children.description: The parent node to group renewal related settings.

children.name: ErrorCode
children.description: If certificate renew fails, this node provide the last hresult code during renew process.

children.name: LastRenewalAttemptTime
children.description: Time of last attempted renew

children.name: ROBOSupport
children.description: Optional. Notify the client whether enrollment server supports ROBO auto certificate renew. NOTE: This flag is only needed to the device which is MDM enrolled via On-premise authentication method. For MDM enrolled with federated authentication, ROBO is the only supported renewal method. If the server sets this node value to be false or delete this node for federated enrolled device, the configuration will fail with OMA DM error code 405.

children.name: RenewNow
children.description: Initiate a renew now

children.name: RenewPeriod
children.description: Specify the number of days prior to the enrollment cert expiration to prompt the user to renew.

children.name: RetryAfterExpiryInterval
children.description: How long after the enrollment cert has expiried to keep trying to renew

children.name: RetryInterval
children.description: Optional. This parameter specifies retry interval when previous renew failed (in days). It applies to both manual cert renewal and ROBO cert renewal. Retry schedule will stop at cert expiration date. For ROBO renewal failure, the client retries the renewal periodically until the device reaches the certificate expiration date. This parameter specifies the waiting period for ROBO renewal retries. For manual retry failure, there are no built-in retries. The user can retry later. At the next scheduled certificate renewal retry period, the device prompts the credential dialog again. The default value is 7 and the valid values are 1 – 1000 AND =< RenewalPeriod, otherwise it will result in errors. Value type is an integer.

children.name: ServerURL
children.description: Optional. Specifies the cert renewal server URL which is the discovery server.

children.name: Status
children.description: Show the latest action status for this certificate. Supported values are one of the following: 0 – Not started. 1 – Renewal in progress. 2 – Renewal succeeded. 3 – Renewal failed.

nodes.name: ROOT
nodes.description: This store holds only root (self-signed) certificates.
